findings.title: Webhook signature regex lost start/end anchors, allowing partial matches to pass validation
findings.evidence: Pattern changed from /^sha256=[a-f0-9]{64}$/ to /sha256=[a-f0-9]{64}/ (raw diff line 6-7). signaturePattern.test(header) in hasValidShape() now matches any header that merely contains a sha256= hex substring anywhere, so attacker-controlled prefixes/suffixes (e.g. extra chars, extra hex digits, or junk wrapped around a forged token) pass the shape check that gates signature verification.

notes: The Diff Drift report correctly identifies the single high-severity flag and matches the raw diff exactly; only one file (src/verifySignature.ts) changed.
No file was marked 'Skipped — file too large to analyze' in this packet.

The function is named hasValidShape, suggesting it is a shape/format precheck; even so, removing anchors weakens an authentication-related guard and should be restored to /^sha256=[a-f0-9]{64}$/ before trust.

evaluator.note: Benchmark v4 blind run against the engine-v2 binary; packet-only context.
